notes: Iteration 11 revised plan. Phase 0 (data integrity) and Phase 1.1 (patching spot-check) COMPLETED. Remaining: Phase 1.2 (city-country patching), Phase 1.3 (first-letter L24 patching), Phase 2 (writing revisions), Phase 2.7 (final validation), Phase 3 (optional). Total remaining: ~1-3 GPU-hours, ~9 wall-clock hours.

tasks.name: Phase 1.2: City-Country Activation Patching (with Probe Quality Caveat)
tasks.description: Run activation patching for city-country hierarchy at L24 despite low probe quality (F1=0.73). This addresses the gap where the highest-absorption hierarchy (45.1%) was excluded from causal testing without explanation. (1) Load city-country probes and identify parent-child feature pairs from Gemma Scope L24 16k. (2) For each city-country entity where absorption is detected: zero child feature, measure parent probe recovery. (3) Run same control condition (zero random non-child feature). (4) Report Cohen's d and p-value with explicit caveat: 'Probe quality (F1=0.73) falls below the relaxed gate; false negatives in the probe confound the recovery measurement.' (5) Interpret: if d > 0.3, competitive exclusion likely present but measurement is noisy; if d < 0.3, cannot distinguish from noise given probe quality. FALLBACK: If patching fails due to insufficient absorbed pairs at L24, write documented exclusion paragraph for Section 5.2 instead.

pilot.pass_criteria: Patching completes successfully. Result reported with probe quality caveat. If d > 0.3: strengthens universal claim. If d < 0.3: documented as consequence of probe quality.

visualizations.description: City-country patching results with probe quality context

tasks.name: Phase 1.3: First-Letter Activation Patching at L24
tasks.description: Re-run first-letter activation patching at L24 to fix Figure 4 layer mismatch (currently first-letter at L12, RAVEL at L24). (1) Load Gemma 2 2B + Gemma Scope L24 16k SAE. (2) Identify first-letter parent-child feature pairs using sae-spelling methodology at L24. (3) For each first-letter absorbed pair: zero child feature, measure parent probe recovery. (4) Run control condition (zero random non-child feature). (5) Compute Cohen's d and p-value. (6) Generate updated Figure 4 with all three panels at L24 (first-letter, city-continent, city-language). Use matplotlib, publication-quality (300 DPI, tight layout, seaborn palette). Output: exp/results/phase1/firstletter_l24_patching.json + exp/results/figures/fig4_patching_l24.pdf.

pilot.pass_criteria: Patching completes at L24. Recovery qualitatively consistent with L12 result (positive recovery, d > 0.5). Figure 4 generated with all three panels at L24.

visualizations.description: Figure 4: Three-panel activation patching comparison at L24

tasks.name: Phase 2.1-2.6: Comprehensive Writing Revisions
tasks.description: All writing revisions combined into one task, executed AFTER Phase 0 data integrity and Phase 1 verification. (2.1) Reframe probe degradation: linear fit primary, quadratic exploratory; emphasize monotonicity (rho=-1.0) over R^2; note binary-to-multiclass extrapolation limitation; reframe as 'directional evidence' not 'quantitative decomposition'. (2.2) Section 7.2 repetition: remove all re-presented numbers, keep implications only, reference Section 5. Section 8: one-sentence summary. (2.3) Figure 5: render both distributions visible (different colors, alpha transparency, or separate panels); reconcile N=1471 vs N=1464. Figure 6: reconcile p=0.063 (figure) vs p=0.041 (text) by checking statistical test used. (2.4) Abstract: reduce to 5-7 key numbers; move remainder to introduction. (2.5) Style fixes: 'dominant'->'widely adopted'; 'amplify'->'are relevant to'; add SAEBench dual-inclusion acknowledgment in Related Work; add code availability statement; pin library versions (SAELens v6, TransformerLens, sae-spelling). (2.6) Update proposal.md to match per-token canonical numbers from Phase 0. Use data_manifest.json as the authoritative cross-check. Output: updated paper.md + proposal.md.

pilot.pass_criteria: validate_integration.py passes on final paper.md. No CI inversions. No aggregation contradictions. No stale headline numbers. Section 7.2 contains zero re-presented numbers. Abstract has <= 7 numerical claims.

visualizations.description: Figure 6 (fixed): architecture comparison with reconciled p-value

tasks.name: Phase 2.7: Final Validation Pass
tasks.description: Run validate_integration.py on the final paper.md after all corrections from Phase 0-2. This is the quality gate before declaring the iteration complete. (1) Run validate_integration.py. (2) If any mismatches remain, fix them. (3) Generate final validation_report.json documenting: total claims checked, claims passing, claims fixed this iteration, remaining known limitations. (4) Run a manual spot-check of 5 randomly selected claims from data_manifest.json, tracing each to source JSON. Output: exp/results/phase2/final_validation_report.json.

pilot.pass_criteria: validate_integration.py exits 0. Zero CI inversions. Zero aggregation contradictions. All 5 spot-checked claims trace correctly to source.

tasks.name: Phase 3.1: City-Continent Probe Degradation (Optional)
tasks.description: OPTIONAL: Validate cross-domain transferability of probe degradation curve. Currently, the curve is built from first-letter binary probes only, then extrapolated to multi-class RAVEL probes. (1) Train city-continent multi-class probe at L24 (if not already cached). (2) Degrade probe to 4 F1 levels (0.95, 0.85, 0.75, 0.65) using Gaussian noise injection with calibrated epsilon. (3) At each degraded F1 level, measure city-continent absorption rate using the degraded probe. (4) Plot absorption vs F1 and compute slope. (5) Compare slope with first-letter degradation curve slope. If within 2x: extrapolation validated. If >2x different: reframe Section 4.6 as domain-specific. Output: exp/results/phase3/city_continent_probe_degradation.json. NOTE: This task exceeds 60 minutes because multi-class probe degradation requires more calibration points than binary probes.

pilot.pass_criteria: Probe degradation at >= 3 F1 levels completed. Absorption rate monotonically increases as F1 decreases. Slope comparison with first-letter documented.

visualizations.description: Probe degradation: absorption vs F1 (city-continent vs first-letter overlay)

tasks.name: Phase 3.2: Rate-Distortion Three-Factor Predictor Validation (Optional)
tasks.description: OPTIONAL: Test H9 -- per-pair absorption probability predicted by cos_sim(decoder_parent, decoder_child) x P(child|parent) / R(parent). (1) For each hierarchy, compute per-pair features: (a) cos_sim between parent and child decoder vectors, (b) co-occurrence P(child|parent) from training data statistics, (c) reconstruction importance R(parent) = MSE contribution. (2) Fit three-factor model on pooled data from all hierarchies. (3) Evaluate: Spearman rho on held-out pairs (leave-one-hierarchy-out cross-validation). (4) If rho > 0.5: genuine predictive power. If rho < 0.3: document as additional negative result alongside H4 and H5. Output: exp/results/phase3/rate_distortion_validation.json.

pilot.pass_criteria: Three-factor model fit completed. Spearman rho and p-value computed. If rho > 0.5: H9 supported. If rho < 0.3: negative result documented.

visualizations.description: Predicted vs. observed per-pair absorption probability
